audited_claim_quote: BatteryFormer accurately reproduces the experimental average voltages for seven representative lithium-ion cathode materials
caveats_that_travel: This verdict concerns absolute average voltages on three anchored couples only; the model's in-frame (reference-scale) performance and its other predictions are NOT tested here.

QME's own local DFT bench FAILED its pre-registered single-offset calibration gate (sd 0.31 V, n=4; source: d1_corrected_offsets.json in github.com/Krishnatejavepa/qme-paper-validation, regenerable) and contributes nothing to this audit; the comparison is paper-vs-experimental-anchor only, and QME voltage language remains ranking-only.

On the compounds where model, database reference, and experiment could previously be compared (QME's Na-cathode study, arXiv:2606.23725), Materials Project PBE+U reference voltages sat about 0.54 V below experiment; this audit's subject trained on MP Battery Explorer labels, and the anchored-subset result here neither confirms nor refutes that systematic for its full training domain.
The LiCoO2 anchor carries the pre-flagged range-match caveat: the paper does not define its (de)lithiation range, so a definitional mismatch can neither be demonstrated nor ruled out from its text.

The three transcribed predictions are printed figure labels (u_v = 0.005 V); the paper's SI Table S7 was not retrievable by automated means, and author-provided values supersede these transcriptions under the registered uncertainty rule.

datapoints.anchor_source: Padhi, Nanjundaswamy, Goodenough, J. Electrochem. Soc. 144, 1188 (1997)
standard 3.9-4.1 V plateau window; range-match caveat pre-flagged in registration
Thackeray et al., Mater. Res. Bull. 18, 461 (1983); Ohzuku et al., JES 137, 769 (1990); average of the two plateaus

exclusions.sensitivity_analysis: no datapoint excluded, so verdict-with-and-without does not apply

x1_range_mismatch.basis: The paper's methods text does not define the (de)lithiation range for its average-voltage labels (it states only that training data came from the Materials Project Battery Explorer with a -1 to 7 V selection window). The registered x1 rule requires a mismatch demonstrable from the paper's methods text BEFORE value comparison; silence does not demonstrate a mismatch, so no datapoint is excluded. The LiCoO2 pre-flag is retained as a disclosed caveat in the verdict artifact.
x1_range_mismatch.quote: All training data used in this work were sourced from the Battery Explorer module provided by the Materials Project, with the selected voltage range for cathode materials between -1 to 7 V.

x2_unreadable_value.basis: all three anchored values are printed labels, u_v = 0.005 V, far below the 0.10 V bar

negative_assertions: No claim that the model is refuted as a model class.
No claim of author misconduct or error; validating against computed references is field-standard practice.
No absolute-voltage claims by QME's own bench.
A FAIL would be published with the same standing as a PASS; this verdict is published under the registered verdict policy either way.

primary_metric.definition: MAE = mean |V_predicted(paper) - V_exp(Tier-A anchor)| over included datapoints

rule_applied: MAE = 0.1333 V; class boundaries PASS<0.15, FAIL>=0.30; uncertainty envelope [0.1283, 0.1383] V stays in one class

secondary.mp_reference_decomposition: not retrieved: the generator performs no network access by design, and no retrieval is recorded in the curation log; the registered fallback (omission with disclosure) applies. The decomposition is secondary and never verdict-deciding (registered section 3)
secondary.paper_experiment_labels_vs_anchors_note: The paper's own experiment bars (LiFePO4 3.4, LiCoO2 3.9, LiMn2O4 4.0) differ from the committed Tier-A anchors (3.45, 4.0, 4.05) by 0.05 to 0.10 V; reference-choice scatter, recorded as context, never a verdict input.

subject.title: A Universal Machine Learning Framework Driven by Artificial Intelligence for Ion Battery Cathode Material Design

verdict_scope: anchored subset only (n=3 of 7 Figure-6a compounds; see caveats_that_travel[0])
